skill.name: quieter
skill.description: Tone down overly bold or visually aggressive designs. Reduces intensity while maintaining design quality and impact.
skill.summary: Refine loud visual designs into calmer, more sophisticated interfaces without losing hierarchy or usability.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and network reconnaissance keywords, but the reviewed file contains design guidance only. The matches occur in prose about visual intensity, gray colors, and typography, with no scripts, commands, network calls, cryptographic code, or prompt injection attempts found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches are prose in a design skill, including text about visual intensity, color approaches, and quiet design. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language design instructions, not code. The file has no implementation surface for cryptography or data handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: The reported network reconnaissance matches are typography and hierarchy guidance. No evidence found of port scanning, host discovery, network requests, or external command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is visual design prose about grays and hierarchy. There are no URLs, socket operations, command examples, or reconnaissance instructions.

content.value_statement: Many interfaces become hard to use when color, scale, motion, and visual weight all compete for attention. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code make designs calmer while preserving clarity, hierarchy, and impact.
actual_capabilities: Identifies sources of excessive visual intensity in interface designs.
Guides color desaturation, palette simplification, and gentler contrast choices.
Recommends typography, spacing, border, and visual weight reductions.
Helps reduce decorative animation while keeping useful interaction feedback.
Checks that refined designs remain functional, distinctive, readable, and polished.
limitations: It provides design guidance and depends on the host agent to inspect or edit project files.
It requires existing design context or a compatible preparation workflow.
It does not generate a complete design system by itself.
It cannot verify visual quality without rendered screens or project context.

use_cases.title: Calm a Marketing Page
use_cases.description: Reduce loud colors, oversized elements, and decorative effects while keeping the page persuasive.

use_cases.title: Improve Product UI Focus
use_cases.description: Make a busy dashboard easier to scan by reducing competing visual weight and excessive contrast.

use_cases.title: Polish Frontend Implementation
use_cases.description: Guide code edits that soften typography, spacing, borders, colors, and motion in an existing app.

prompt_templates.title: Quick UI Tone Down
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quieter on this component. Reduce visual intensity while keeping the main action clear.

prompt_templates.title: Review Current Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quieter to review this page. Identify the strongest sources of visual intensity before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Product Surface
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quieter on the dashboard. Preserve information hierarchy, improve scanability, and reduce unnecessary color and motion.

prompt_templates.title: Systematic Visual Restraint Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quieter across this feature. Audit color, type, spacing, borders, effects, motion, and composition, then implement a restrained refinement plan.

output_examples.input: A landing page uses bright gradients, heavy shadows, large headings, and animated decorative elements.
output: The refined direction keeps one strong headline and one accent color.
Backgrounds become simpler, shadows become lighter, and decorative motion is removed.
Spacing and typography create hierarchy without making every element compete.

output_examples.input: A dashboard feels loud because every chart, badge, and button uses saturated color.
output: Status colors are reserved for meaningful states.
Neutral surfaces carry most of the layout.
Typography and spacing guide attention before color is used.

output_examples.input: A component has oversized controls, thick borders, and strong hover animations.
output: Controls are resized to match their context.
Borders use lower opacity and cleaner alignment.
Hover feedback remains visible but uses shorter, gentler motion.

best_practices: Start by identifying what must remain visually strong before reducing intensity elsewhere.
Use fewer colors and let spacing, type, and alignment carry more hierarchy.
Verify the quieter design still supports usability, readability, and brand character.
anti_patterns: Do not turn every design into grayscale or remove all personality.
Do not make all elements small, light, or visually equal.
Do not remove functional affordances only to achieve a softer appearance.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps reduce excessive visual intensity in user interfaces while preserving quality, hierarchy, and usability.

faq.question: Is this only for minimal design?
faq.answer: No. It supports expressive designs, but guides them toward restraint, clarity, and better focus.

faq.question: Can it edit frontend code?
faq.answer: It can guide a compatible coding agent to edit colors, typography, spacing, effects, and motion in project files.

faq.question: Does it replace a design system?
faq.answer: No. It works best with existing product goals, brand direction, components, and rendered context.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the target component, page, or feature, plus any product context or screenshots that show the current design.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill contains design instructions only and no executable code, network behavior, or data access.
